verses.latin_vulgate: Quam pulchra es, amica mea ! quam pulchra es !  Oculi tui columbarum,  absque eo quod intrinsecus latet.  Capilli tui sicut greges caprarum  quæ ascenderunt de monte Galaad.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנָּ֨ךְ יָפָ֤ה רַעְיָתִי֙ הִנָּ֣ךְ יָפָ֔ה עֵינַ֣יִךְ יוֹנִ֔ים מִבַּ֖עַד לְצַמָּתֵ֑ךְ שַׂעְרֵךְ֙ כְּעֵ֣דֶר הָֽעִזִּ֔ים שֶׁגָּלְשׁ֖וּ מֵהַ֥ר גִּלְעָֽד׃
verses.king_james: Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; thou hast doves’ eyes within thy locks: thy hair is as a flock of goats, that appear from mount Gilead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dentes tui sicut greges tonsarum  quæ ascenderunt de lavacro ;  omnes gemellis fœtibus,  et sterilis non est inter eas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁנַּ֙יִךְ֙ כְּעֵ֣דֶר הַקְּצוּב֔וֹת שֶׁעָל֖וּ מִן־הָרַחְצָ֑ה שֶׁכֻּלָּם֙ מַתְאִימ֔וֹת וְשַׁכֻּלָ֖ה אֵ֥ין בָּהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Thy teeth are like a flock of sheep that are even shorn, which came up from the washing; whereof every one bear twins, and none is barren among them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut vitta coccinea labia tua,  et eloquium tuum dulce.  Sicut fragmen mali punici, ita genæ tuæ,  absque eo quod intrinsecus latet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּח֤וּט הַשָּׁנִי֙ שִׂפְתוֹתַ֔יִךְ וּמִדְבָּרֵ֖ךְ נָאוֶ֑ה כְּפֶ֤לַח הָֽרִמּוֹן֙ רַקָּתֵ֔ךְ מִבַּ֖עַד לְצַמָּתֵֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Thy lips are like a thread of scarlet, and thy speech is comely: thy temples are like a piece of a pomegranate within thy locks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut turris David collum tuum,  quæ ædificata est cum propugnaculis ;  mille clypei pendent ex ea,  omnis armatura fortium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּמִגְדַּ֤ל דָּוִיד֙ צַוָּארֵ֔ךְ בָּנ֖וּי לְתַלְפִּיּ֑וֹת אֶ֤לֶף הַמָּגֵן֙ תָּל֣וּי עָלָ֔יו כֹּ֖ל שִׁלְטֵ֥י הַגִּבֹּרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thy neck is like the tower of David builded for an armoury, whereon there hang a thousand bucklers, all shields of mighty men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Duo ubera tua sicut duo hinnuli,  capreæ gemelli, qui pascuntur in liliis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁנֵ֥י שָׁדַ֛יִךְ כִּשְׁנֵ֥י עֳפָרִ֖ים תְּאוֹמֵ֣י צְבִיָּ֑ה הָרוֹעִ֖ים בַּשּׁוֹשַׁנִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thy two breasts are like two young roes that are twins, which feed among the lilies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Donec aspiret dies, et inclinentur umbræ,  vadam ad montem myrrhæ, et ad collem thuris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֤ד שֶׁיָּפ֙וּחַ֙ הַיּ֔וֹם וְנָ֖סוּ הַצְּלָלִ֑ים אֵ֤לֶךְ לִי֙ אֶל־הַ֣ר הַמּ֔וֹר וְאֶל־גִּבְעַ֖ת הַלְּבוֹנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, I will get me to the mountain of myrrh, and to the hill of frankincense.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tota pulchra es, amica mea,  et macula non est in te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֻּלָּ֤ךְ יָפָה֙ רַעְיָתִ֔י וּמ֖וּם אֵ֥ין בָּֽךְ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Thou art all fair, my love; there is no spot in thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veni de Libano, sponsa mea :  veni de Libano, veni, coronaberis :  de capite Amana, de vertice Sanir et Hermon,  de cubilibus leonum, de montibus pardorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִתִּ֤י מִלְּבָנוֹן֙ כַּלָּ֔ה אִתִּ֖י מִלְּבָנ֣וֹן תָּב֑וֹאִי תָּשׁ֣וּרִי ׀ מֵרֹ֣אשׁ אֲמָנָ֗ה מֵרֹ֤אשׁ שְׂנִיר֙ וְחֶרְמ֔וֹן מִמְּעֹנ֣וֹת אֲרָי֔וֹת מֵֽהַרְרֵ֖י נְמֵרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Come with me from Lebanon, my spouse, with me from Lebanon: look from the top of Amana, from the top of Shenir and Hermon, from the lions’ dens, from the mountains of the leopards.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vulnerasti cor meum, soror mea, sponsa ;  vulnerasti cor meum in uno oculorum tuorum,  et in uno crine colli tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִבַּבְתִּ֖נִי אֲחֹתִ֣י כַלָּ֑ה לִבַּבְתִּ֙נִי֙ (באחד) [בְּאַחַ֣ת] מֵעֵינַ֔יִךְ בְּאַחַ֥ד עֲנָ֖ק מִצַּוְּרֹנָֽיִךְ׃
verses.king_james: Thou hast ravished my heart, my sister, my spouse; thou hast ravished my heart with one of thine eyes, with one chain of thy neck.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam pulchræ sunt mammæ tuæ, soror mea sponsa !  pulchriora sunt ubera tua vino,  et odor unguentorum tuorum super omnia aromata.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־יָּפ֥וּ דֹדַ֖יִךְ אֲחֹתִ֣י כַלָּ֑ה מַה־טֹּ֤בוּ דֹדַ֙יִךְ֙ מִיַּ֔יִן וְרֵ֥יחַ שְׁמָנַ֖יִךְ מִכׇּל־בְּשָׂמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: How fair is thy love, my sister, my spouse! how much better is thy love than wine! and the smell of thine ointments than all spices!

verses.latin_vulgate: Favus distillans labia tua, sponsa ;  mel et lac sub lingua tua :  et odor vestimentorum tuorum sicut odor thuris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֹ֛פֶת תִּטֹּ֥פְנָה שִׂפְתוֹתַ֖יִךְ כַּלָּ֑ה דְּבַ֤שׁ וְחָלָב֙ תַּ֣חַת לְשׁוֹנֵ֔ךְ וְרֵ֥יחַ שַׂלְמֹתַ֖יִךְ כְּרֵ֥יחַ לְבָנֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Thy lips, O my spouse, drop as the honeycomb: honey and milk are under thy tongue; and the smell of thy garments is like the smell of Lebanon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hortus conclusus soror mea, sponsa,  hortus conclusus, fons signatus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֥ן ׀ נָע֖וּל אֲחֹתִ֣י כַלָּ֑ה גַּ֥ל נָע֖וּל מַעְיָ֥ן חָתֽוּם׃
verses.king_james: A garden inclosed is my sister, my spouse; a spring shut up, a fountain sealed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Emissiones tuæ paradisus malorum punicorum,  cum pomorum fructibus, cypri cum nardo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁלָחַ֙יִךְ֙ פַּרְדֵּ֣ס רִמּוֹנִ֔ים עִ֖ם פְּרִ֣י מְגָדִ֑ים כְּפָרִ֖ים עִם־נְרָדִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thy plants are an orchard of pomegranates, with pleasant fruits; camphire, with spikenard,

verses.latin_vulgate: Nardus et crocus, fistula et cinnamomum,  cum universis lignis Libani ;  myrrha et aloë, cum omnibus primis unguentis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֵ֣רְדְּ ׀ וְכַרְכֹּ֗ם קָנֶה֙ וְקִנָּמ֔וֹן עִ֖ם כׇּל־עֲצֵ֣י לְבוֹנָ֑ה מֹ֚ר וַאֲהָל֔וֹת עִ֖ם כׇּל־רָאשֵׁ֥י בְשָׂמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Spikenard and saffron; calamus and cinnamon, with all trees of frankincense; myrrh and aloes, with all the chief spices:

verses.latin_vulgate: Fons hortorum, puteus aquarum viventium,  quæ fluunt impetu de Libano.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַעְיַ֣ן גַּנִּ֔ים בְּאֵ֖ר מַ֣יִם חַיִּ֑ים וְנֹזְלִ֖ים מִן־לְבָנֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: A fountain of gardens, a well of living waters, and streams from Lebanon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surge, aquilo, et veni, auster :  perfla hortum meum, et fluant aromata illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ע֤וּרִי צָפוֹן֙ וּב֣וֹאִי תֵימָ֔ן הָפִ֥יחִי גַנִּ֖י יִזְּל֣וּ בְשָׂמָ֑יו יָבֹ֤א דוֹדִי֙ לְגַנּ֔וֹ וְיֹאכַ֖ל פְּרִ֥י מְגָדָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Awake, O north wind; and come, thou south; blow upon my garden, that the spices thereof may flow out. Let my beloved come into his garden, and eat his pleasant fruits.
